app.title: Minute Man
app.description: A progressive web app for your range

zeroCalculator.title: Zero Calculator
zeroCalculator.invalidInput: Please fill in at least one offset value
zeroCalculator.helpDiagramAlt: Zero Calculator point of aim and point of impact diagram
zeroCalculator.helpPointAimImpact: Point of Aim is where you intended the shot to land. Point of Impact is where the shot actually landed.
zeroCalculator.helpPointImpactGroup: When measuring Point of Impact, use roughly the center of a group of shots instead of a single hole.
zeroCalculator.helpOffsets: Horizontal Offset is the left or right distance between those two points, and Vertical Offset is the up or down distance between them.

mildotCalculator.title: MilDot Calculator
mildotCalculator.description: Calculate mil size, target size, or distance
mildotCalculator.invalidInput: Please provide at least two values
mildotCalculator.helpIntro: The MilDot Calculator relates three values: Mil Size, Physical Size, and Distance. Enter any two known values and leave the value you want to solve for blank.
mildotCalculator.helpTerms: Mil Size is how large the target appears in your reticle, measured in mils. Physical Size is the real-world size of the target. Distance is the range from you to the target.
mildotCalculator.helpExample: For example, if you know the target's physical size and how many mils it spans in the scope, the calculator can estimate distance.

holdoverCalculator.title: Holdover Calculator
holdoverCalculator.estimateNotice: This is a simplified estimate, not a full ballistic solution.
holdoverCalculator.helpZeroDistance: Zero Distance is where point of aim and point of impact should align.
holdoverCalculator.helpDiagramIntro: Your sight line starts above the barrel, so at very close distances the projectile may still be below your point of aim. The calculator estimates this close-range offset using your zero distance and height over bore.
holdoverCalculator.helpDiagramDisclaimer: This diagram is simplified for clarity and does not represent a full ballistic trajectory.
holdoverCalculator.helpTargetDistance: Target Distance is the current distance to the target you intend to engage.
holdoverCalculator.helpHeightOverBore: Height Over Bore is the vertical distance between the centerline of the optic or sights and the centerline of the barrel.
holdoverCalculator.helpFirearmProfile: Profiles provide sensible default height-over-bore values for common firearm configurations.
holdoverCalculator.helpOutputUnit: Output Unit controls whether the hold is shown as physical offset, MOA, or MRAD.
holdoverCalculator.resultSummary: At {{targetDistance}} {{distanceUnit}} with a {{zeroDistance}} {{distanceUnit}} zero and {{heightOverBore}} {{heightUnit}} sight height, your impact is estimated to be {{impactAmount}} {{outputUnit}} {{impactDirection}}.
holdoverCalculator.disclaimer: This is a simplified estimate based on sight height and zero distance. It does not account for bullet drop, ammunition, velocity, or environmental conditions.

shotTimer.title: Shot Timer
shotTimer.firstShotSensitivityHint: First Shot mode: a shot will stop the timer.
shotTimer.splitSensitivityHint: Split and Par modes: shots are automatically recorded as splits.
shotTimer.noSplits: No splits recorded yet. Start the timer and shots will appear here.
shotTimer.consoleHint: Check the browser console (F12) for more details. Make sure this site is HTTPS (not HTTP).
shotTimer.helpIntro: Shot Timer listens through the microphone while the timer is running and records sharp sound spikes as shots.
shotTimer.helpStartMode: Start Mode controls when the start beep happens: Instant starts immediately, Delayed waits briefly, and Random waits a random interval before the beep.
shotTimer.helpTimerMode: Timer Mode controls what is measured. Split Timer records each detected shot as a split. Par Timer counts down from the par time. First Shot stops on the first detected shot.
shotTimer.helpSensitivity: Sensitivity adjusts the sound threshold for detection. Increase sensitivity if shots are missed, and lower it if background noise is being counted as shots.

rangeConditions.title: Range Conditions
rangeConditions.noData: No weather data available. Enter a location to get started.
rangeConditions.geolocationNotSupported: Geolocation is not supported by your browser
rangeConditions.enterLocation: Please enter a location (ZIP code or city name)
rangeConditions.locationNotFound: Location not found. Please try another search.

settings.title: Settings
settings.sensitivityTestHint: Clap, snap, or make sounds. Adjust the slider to find a comfortable threshold.

about.title: About Minute Man
about.description: A progressive web app for useful utilities at the range.
about.installAndroid: Tap the install button in the navigation bar.
about.installIos: Tap Share, then Add to Home Screen.
about.privacy: This app stores all data locally on your device. No data is collected or sent to any server.
about.buyMeCoffee: Like this app? Buy me a coffee!
about.madeBy: Minute Man is brought to you by
about.contactUs: Need help? Hit us up at
about.installPWADescription: Install this app on your device for faster access and offline use.
about.shareTitle: Share this app with the below QR code.
about.shareQrAlt: QR code for sharing Minute Man

validation.mustBePositive: Value must be greater than zero
